verdict_reason: The disagreement entry on 'veda-vāda-ratāḥ ... nānyad astīti vādinaḥ' has substance. It correctly identifies the doctrinal load: Prabhupada's 'supposed followers' is sharper than Mukundananda's 'presume no higher principle is described in them'; Shankara restricts the critique to 'Vedic sentences which reveal panegyrics, fruits of action and their means' (i.e., kāmya-karma portion); Ramanuja reads 'na anyad asti' as a consequence of attachment, not a metaphysical claim. The explanation correctly states the operative point: 'the verse is therefore not anti-Vedic — it is anti-fixation-on-one-portion-of-the-Vedas.' This is exactly the scoping the engineering layer preserves. PASS.
